entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop (Pennisetum glaucum) grown in semi-arid tropics.

entities.name: DOWNY MILDEW
entities.description: A disease caused by Sclerospora graminicola affecting pearl millet.

entities.name: SCLEROSPORA GRAMINICOLA
entities.description: An obligate oomycete pathogen that causes downy mildew in pearl millet.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Regions characterized by low rainfall and high temperatures where pearl millet is grown.

entities.name: OBLIGATE OOMYCETES
entities.description: Organisms that require a living host to complete their life cycle.

entities.name: HETEROTHALLISM
entities.description: A condition in which an individual is not self-fertile, requiring a partner for sexual reproduction.

entities.name: SEXUAL CROSS COMPATIBILITY
entities.description: The ability of different isolates of a pathogen to mate sexually.

entities.name: GENETIC AND PATHOGENIC VARIABILITY
entities.description: Differences in genetic makeup and virulence within a pathogen population.

entities.name: SPORANGIA
entities.description: Asexual spores produced by the pathogen that facilitate the secondary spread of the disease.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the study of pearl millet downy mildew pathogen populations is focused.

entities.name: SINGLE-CROSS F1 HYBRIDS
entities.description: First-generation hybrids produced by crossing two distinct inbred lines.

entities.name: YIELD LOSSES
entities.description: Reduction in the amount of crop harvested due to disease severity.
